reason: 评论为无中文随机英文/数字堆砌
value:
What a beautiful high quality craftsmanship of a product!  Just got it at  $150 and arrived the next day.  Very easy to setup. Package was simple and to the point.  Perfect monitor light size for my 27" Asus Disgno 2K Monitor.   Love that the lightbar comes with it's own power USB C adopter plug and the USB C that's already attached to the back of the screenbar from the back is super high quality.

Love that you can turn down the light to the lowest dim of 1% and have different light color output for the rear light and the main front monitor light separately.  The round controller is of high quality with a very good weight so it doesn't move that easy when using it.  Touch screen buttons are very sensitive and react instantly to the touch, very premium product.  I decided to install the controller to my wall at arms reach for a clean look as the light will not be used constantly and only be used when I need to so it doesn't need to be on my desk. 

Round controller hub is rechargeable and will hold battery for a long time yet it's charges quick.  I you want the light to turn on when you turn on your computer just connect the lightbar to any open USB/USB C port on you desktop or laptop computer. 

Was it worth it?  Hell fk yeah, every penny because it gives a very true to sunlight color light, easy on the eyes and even when I'm laying/relaxing backwards on my LiberNovo Omni chair, the light doesn't hit my eyes.  The back light is perfect for that nice glow to the wall keeping your eyes alert and not making you drowsy and when you do need that full light it lights your front top desk evenly with a full stream of beautiful comfortable light that you can use for reading, fixing stuff or inspecting objects, etc....

When people said that BenQ is the pioneer of screenbar lighting, they were not joking and this newest version is top tear and the flagship for all intimations out there!

reason: 评论为无中文随机英文/数字堆砌
value:
My Xiaomi light bar came with batteries branded MJ (Mijia) for the puck, but I bought it a couple of years ago, so.. who knows, maybe they have stopped shipping it with them? strange.  I just installed the light bar once, adjusted it so that the light didn't get in my eyes (had no issues), and since then, never had to touch it. Only the puck is used, and only to click on/off, cause the temp color and strength is set up from day one, and doesn't need to be changed. The original batteries are still working after 2 years (but I haven't used it every day, as sometimes I work at the computer when it's light in the room too). 

Yeah, the BenQ is the better option probably, but just to light your desk area, and avoid the eye strain from only the monitor light source, the Xiaomi does its work flawlessly at a fraction of the price, so I'm happy with my choice of going with the Xiaomi.

Hey everyone! Thanks for checking out the video. Hopefully some find it useful if looking for great lighting solutions for your work or gaming stations. We may not do product reviews often here until we get a bit larger, but when I do you can expect it to be products I find useful or relevant to what I do in the photography and digital arts industry. I know the price point may seem a bit steep, but for something that’s built to provide daily comfort and reduce glaring issues like eye strain/fatigue… I’d put my money on it more often than not. I also truly love the fact that it’s quite compact and stays out of the way when not using it. 

For those who may ask, I use the ScreenBar Plus for about 80% of my work day. When I’m dealing with color intricacies I tend to turn most if not all of my office lights off, just a personal preference when getting into that zone. 
What do you think? Over priced or worth it? Those who own one, what do you think about it? I’d love to hear from you guys!

reason: 评论包含固定删除词: test
value:
While I don't have the BenQ light bar I do have a similar device that was less expensive and more basic but achieves the targeted goal. I never use my desktop set up without it now, it made a direct impact to reducing eye strain and light fatigue. I have a 4 way monitor setup with 3 across the bottom and the 4th top center. while this light bar can't cover all the monitors my primary one is the brightest of the bunch so I have it set in-between the middle and top middle.
Mine is also usb powered and controlled via a in-line puck of sorts.. remind me of those biiig in line control thingers back in the early 2000s when MP3 headsets were still being figured out to have controls of some type on them.
I plugged it into a ac powered usb hub so I could use it at any time.. it does not have the backlight function though in my case the 4th monitor support bar is right behind it so it probably wouldn't have gotten used much lol.
I sought out the device mostly as a housemate kept complaining about my room light at night in the summertime when they have their door more open for air.. they are those bat types that want as near as perfect dark as they can get lol.. 
Meanwhile i'm a night owl with ptsd and constant nightmares so I'm usually up late afternoon till early morning, this device lets me have a really /good/ desk light on that is dim enough it doesn't bother said house mate and like the BenQ one has a very sharp cut of its projection/throw. I tried two products, I don't recall who made the other one but it had touched based controls I found temperamental/finicky so the one I stuck with is a Quntis ScreenLiner, it has temperature adjust, brightness adjust and will save w/e your last used settings.

reason: 评论为无中文随机英文/数字堆砌
value:
Ok, I bought one of these. For me it doesn't really work.

1. You can turn the light a few degrees up and down, when it is mounted on the monitor. But in fact when you turn the light down there IS a glare on the monitor, so I have to turn it all the way up. But then I see a small strip of light that directly hits my eyes.

2. The light is too bright, even at darkest adjustment. It is so bright that I can see the reflection of the desk, the keyboard and even myself in the screen.

3. It lights up the frame of the monitor, especially the silver bar of an iMac at the bottom. That lowers the contrast of the image on screen and the actual surrounding in a way I find disturbing.

When I do office work, especially where I wanna see papers on the desk, then I can imagine using this light. Because then the things right in front of me are as important than the things on screen.
But when I do creative work with images I don't wanna have so much light, because it simply isn't necessary just to see the keyboard. And then it takes too much from the image on screen.


What a pity it is so bright. Half of the brightness or even less would be enough.

reason: 评论包含固定删除词: test
value: I was hoping to get an answer to my question, but since most sponsored reviews aren’t genuine, I decided to buy this Lightbar and test it myself. Skeptical would be an understatement, I wondered about the light distribution and whether it would actually help my eyes. My setup isn’t ideal: a small lamp beside my desk, one ceiling light, and mostly working at night, so I wanted something to brighten the space. I was also curious about the auto on/off function, which was a big selling point for me. To my surprise, it worked perfectly right out of the box. I left all settings at default, and the auto on/off and auto-dimming features exceeded my expectations without a single glitch. The light is fantastic. At night my desk is well-lit, and my eyes don’t feel as tired as before. It’s pricey, but the quality and performance it's definitely worth the $189CA.

reason: 评论为无中文随机英文/数字堆砌
value:
The newest BenQ Halo 2 is a beautiful high quality craftsmanship of a product!  Just got it at  $150 and arrived the next day.  Very easy to setup. Package was simple and to the point.  Perfect monitor light size for my 27" Asus Disgno 2K Monitor.   Love that the lightbar comes with it's own power USB C adopter plug and the USB C that's already attached to the back of the screenbar from the back is super high quality.

Love that you can turn down the light to the lowest dim of 1% and have different light color output for the rear light and the main front monitor light separately.  The round controller is of high quality with a very good weight so it doesn't move that easy when using it.  Touch screen buttons are very sensitive and react instantly to the touch, very premium product.  I decided to install the controller to my wall at arms reach for a clean look as the light will not be used constantly and only be used when I need to so it doesn't need to be on my desk. 

Round controller hub is rechargeable and will hold battery for a long time yet it's charges quick.  If you want the light to turn on when you turn on your computer just connect the lightbar to any open USB/USB C port on you desktop or laptop computer. 

Was it worth it?  Hell fk yeah, every penny because it gives a very true to sunlight color light, easy on the eyes and even when I'm laying/relaxing backwards on my LiberNovo Omni chair, the light doesn't hit my eyes.  The back light is perfect for that nice glow to the wall keeping your eyes alert and not making you drowsy and when you do need that full light it lights your front top desk evenly with a full stream of beautiful comfortable light that you can use for reading, fixing stuff or inspecting objects, etc....

When people said that BenQ is the pioneer of screenbar lighting, they were not joking and this newest version is top tear and the flagship for all imitations out there!

value: Great!  This is the first review I've seen with a dual monitor setup.  I have two Dell 27" flat screen displays, and I was worried about whether the light from either bar would encroach on the other display, but it looks fine here in the review.  Even though you had two different brands here to show off the difference, I'm going to go with the Quntis for each.  Hell, you can buy 2 Quntis light bars for the price of 1 BenQ and they look just as good.

reason: 同一工作表内重复评论
value: I'm sorry to curb your enthusiasm: the magnetic connection design is flawed, a fact you'll only notice with time. I bought this product back in October 2020 and, after a year or so of use, the lamp started to frequently flick due to bad contact of the metal plates. I was usually able to fix it by moving the bar a bit, but the problem only got worse and now, after two years, it's almost impossible to use the lamp. I suspect this problem was caused by the metal cylindrical body of the lamp having its shape microscopically distorted due to weather variations so that now it won't fit perfectly with the monitor support, affecting the magnetic connection and the proper touching of metal contacts. I'm a big fan of Xiaomi in general, but in this case, the experience was very disappointing!

value:
In FY22/23+ (probably earlier)
Apart from their own utility, all devices should have some basics in regards to charging and any controlling devices.
- Cable to Device should be able to removable, shouldn't be integrated (so we can change it when need be)
- Cable to Device should be USB C to USB C (latest standards)
- Almost all Controlling Devices(Wireless Dial for example) should have a rechargeable battery (with USB C to charge it)
 [ There might be some exceptions for extremely low power devices(which is visible by the battery count/size - example CR 2023) or use-cases(frequency of use/lack of time availability to recharge) ]

reason: 评论为无中文随机英文/数字堆砌
value:
What a beautiful high quality craftsmanship of a product!  Just got it at  $150 and arrived the next day.  Very easy to setup. Package was simple and to the point.  Perfect monitor light size for my 27" Asus Disgno 2K Monitor.   Love that the lightbar comes with it's own power USB C adopter plug and the USB C that's already attached to the back of the screenbar from the back is super high quality.

Love that you can turn down the light to the lowest dim of 1% and have different light color output for the rear light and the main front monitor light separately.  The round controller is of high quality with a very good weight so it doesn't move that easy when using it.  Touch screen buttons are very sensitive and react instantly to the touch, very premium product.  I decided to install the controller to my wall at arms reach for a clean look as the light will not be used constantly and only be used when I need to so it doesn't need to be on my desk. 

Round controller hub is rechargeable and will hold battery for a long time yet it's charges quick.  I you want the light to turn on when you turn on your computer just connect the lightbar to any open USB/USB C port on you desktop or laptop computer. 

Was it worth it?  Hell fk yeah, every penny because it gives a very true to sunlight color light, easy on the eyes and even when I'm laying/relaxing backwards on my LiberNovo Omni chair, the light doesn't hit my eyes.  The back light is perfect for that nice glow to the wall keeping your eyes alert and not making you drowsy and when you do need that full light it lights your front top desk evenly with a full stream of beautiful comfortable light that you can use for reading, fixing stuff or inspecting objects, etc....

When people said that BenQ is the pioneer of screenbar lighting, they were not joking and this newest version is top tear and the flagship for all intimations out there!

reason: 评论包含固定删除词: first
value:
I wanted to try out the Xiaomi light bar first, but almost all of the main sellers on Amazon had poor reviews and higher than RRP prices.

So, I bit the bullet and went for the BenQ Halo instead.

I don't regret my purchase so far, and I find the back light a lot more useful than I initially anticipated.

Yes, you could absolutely buy the Xiaomi light bar for the desk, and then get a cheap LED lamp to put behind the monitor as biased/ambient lighting.

However, I don't have heaps of space behind my monitor and nor do I have a spare socket.

The BenQ Halo handles everything in one solution, which saves space and prevents extra clutter.

Bias lighting to save your eyes on the longer run is worth a 1000 dollars more let alone 100. Consider that you don't need to cut led light strips and stick it to the back of your monitor for backlight, and then control its brightness and adjust it seperately... and then worry about removing it, selling your old monitor, buying a new monitor, sticking it again...

And the webcam attachment is handy...
I will choose the Mi for the regular screenlight, but the halo is a leg up; I'm surprised Xiaomi have not followed up with this rather inexpensive feature...

value:
What a beautiful high quality craftsmanship of a product!  Just got it at  $150 and arrived the next day.  Very easy to setup. Package was simple and to the point.  Perfect monitor light size for my 27" Asus Disgno 2K Monitor.   Love that the lightbar comes with it's own power USB C adopter plug and the USB C that's already attached to the back of the screenbar from the back is super high quality.

Love that you can turn down the light to the lowest dim of 1% and have different light color output for the rear light and the main front monitor light separately.  The round controller is of high quality with a very good weight so it doesn't move that easy when using it.  Touch screen buttons are very sensitive and react instantly to the touch, very premium product.  I decided to install the controller to my wall at arms reach for a clean look as the light will not be used constantly and only be used when I need to so it doesn't need to be on my desk. 

Round controller hub is rechargeable and will hold battery for a long time yet it's charges quick.  I you want the light to turn on when you turn on your computer just connect the lightbar to any open USB/USB C port on you desktop or laptop computer. 

Was it worth it?  Hell fk yeah, every penny because it gives a very true to sunlight color light, easy on the eyes and even when I'm laying/relaxing backwards on my LiberNovo Omni chair, the light doesn't hit my eyes.  The back light is perfect for that nice glow to the wall keeping your eyes alert and not making you drowsy and when you do need that full light it lights your front top desk evenly with a full stream of beautiful comfortable light that you can use for reading, fixing stuff or inspecting objects, etc....

When people said that BenQ is the pioneer of screenbar lighting, they were not joking and this newest version is top tear and the flagship for all intimations out there!

reason: 评论包含固定删除词: test
value: I was hoping to get an answer to my question, but since most sponsored reviews aren’t genuine, I decided to buy this Lightbar and test it myself. Skeptical would be an understatement, I wondered about the light distribution and whether it would actually help my eyes. My setup isn’t ideal: a small lamp beside my desk, one ceiling light, and mostly working at night, so I wanted something to brighten the space. I was also curious about the auto on/off function, which was a big selling point for me. To my surprise, it worked perfectly right out of the box. I left all settings at default, and the auto on/off and auto-dimming features exceeded my expectations without a single glitch. The light is fantastic. At night my desk is well-lit, and my eyes don’t feel as tired as before. It’s pricey, but the quality and performance it's definitely worth the $189CA.

EDIT: Managed to fit it on my monitor, just had to adjust the weight position at the end to go upwards and it stays on pretty well. Not amazing, still a bit wobbly but at least it doesn't fall down at a slightest touch!

Bought 2 Quntis - one for me and one for my wife. Her monitor has a more geometric shape so it fits perfectly while on my Asus TUF VG34VQL1B it just doesn't stay solid at all - too much of a curve and the counterweight is too small for it to be held securely, any tap on the lamp would cause it to fall down. One could potentially add something to the back of the counterweight to increase the weight further. I have a feeling BenQ would do better for curved monitor tops than the Quntis. Both lamps are on the same level of good equipment tho!

value:
My biggest concern is longevity and failure rates. I've looked up a lot of them, but it seems like there's an alarming number of them that fail within one year of use (even BenQ's seem to have some, albeit lower rates compared to the rest).

Xiaomi's light bar seems to have the highest failure rates among the light bars from a reputable brand (i.e. not counting the Amazon/Aliexpress/no-brand models that all look like the same thing), with many reviews mentioning that the build quality of the parts you can see on the outside is very high and feels very premium, but the internals are complete garbage and tend to fail within a few months for no apparent reason.

Anyway, this video cements my belief that curved monitors are an unnecessary, gimmicky invention with negligible benefits and were made to solve a non-existent problem and get more money out of customers. I bought one just to try and had used it for over a year before selling it and switching back to my flat display (with the same specs), and while I don't have problems with the monitor itself, I don't think it has any major advantage over a flat display with the same specs. If anything, its cons outweigh its pros.

Love that the lightbar comes with it's own power USB C adopter plug and the USB C that's already attached to the back of the screenbar from the back is super high quality.

Love that you can turn down the light to the lowest dim of 1% and have different light color output for the rear light and the main front monitor light separately.  The round controller is of high quality with a very good weight so it doesn't move that easy when using it.  Touch screen buttons are very sensitive and react instantly to the touch, very premium product.  I decided to install the controller to my wall at arms reach for a clean look as the light will not be used constantly and only be used when I need to so it doesn't need to be on my desk. 

Round controller hub is rechargeable and will hold battery for a long time yet it's charges quick.  I you want the light to turn on when you turn on your computer just connect the lightbar to any open USB/USB C port on you desktop or laptop computer. 

Was it worth it?  Hell fk yeah, every penny because it gives a very true to sunlight color light, easy on the eyes and even when I'm laying/relaxing backwards on my LiberNovo Omni chair, the light doesn't hit my eyes.  The back light is perfect for that nice glow to the wall keeping your eyes alert and not making you drowsy and when you do need that full light it lights your front top desk evenly with a full stream of beautiful comfortable light that you can use for reading, fixing stuff or inspecting objects, etc....

When people said that BenQ is the pioneer of screenbar lighting, they were not joking and this newest version is top tear and the flagship for all intimations out there!

reason: 同一工作表内重复评论
value: I'm sorry to curb your enthusiasm: the magnetic connection design is flawed, a fact you'll only notice with time. I bought this product back in October 2020 and, after a year or so of use, the lamp started to frequently flick due to bad contact of the metal plates. I was usually able to fix it by moving the bar a bit, but the problem only got worse and now, after two years, it's almost impossible to use the lamp. I suspect this problem was caused by the metal cylindrical body of the lamp having its shape microscopically distorted due to weather variations so that now it won't fit perfectly with the monitor support, affecting the magnetic connection and the proper touching of metal contacts. I'm a big fan of Xiaomi in general, but in this case, the experience was very disappointing!

value:
Hey David Zhang, how about a challenge: Using Raspberry 0 w as your main PC for a day or three?

I'm sugesting this because in 2012, Chris from Explaining Computers used Rpi2 as his PC for a week. Now in 2021, covid made more jobs change to the online model, and here in Brazil the prices of computers got very high. As i'm using my Rpi4 for main computer for a whole year, ive had a really good experience on manjaro with sway etc. Many wonder what can be used or not in Rpi0w, so this test could be great. Some details to make the sugestion working:

-SYSTEM: raspberry OS. Maybe you get a better experience with arch, dietPi or even Gentoo idk.

-DESKTOP ENVIROMENT: change the LXDE to sway or i3 (novaspirit has a great tutorial on this). Maybe 540p resolution would be better

-PROGRAMS: abiword instead of libreoffice, gimp?, firefox with lightweight extensions or some terminal browser (i can sugest more tips on this topic)

-HARDWARE RECOMENDATIONS: SSD or HD with external powering instead of the microSD memory, a good microSD or even leepspvideo's PiSafe's eMMC to boot, keyboard with dongle, cable management, and of course a great usb hub or the GPIO shild with more USBs, overclock and a little heatsink
 
-CHALLENGES: try typing some text, light image and audio editing, youtube playback with h264ify or pasting the link in vlc, gaming (even light PS1 games works), benchmarks, light versions of sites like gmail and facebook, video or audio conferences, multitasking in general with htop, wine, DRM, ssh, cmatrix of course, etc

value:
Some Additional Notes:
1. Alternative mount method on the Quntis: Another way to mount on rounded/curved screens is like this, https://i.imgur.com/HMBP9wx.jpg. But as mentioned, the counterbalance is very light, so it may or may not be super stable unless you weigh it down with the USB cable this way. And it doesn't work on my thicker monitors.
2) Are these Flicker-Free? In short, I don't think flicker is an issue on either of these specific models. But other models may differ. Long answer; The marketing label "flicker free" LEDs is a bit of a misnomer. It usually only means no "perceivable" flicker, sometimes in the range as high as 10,000Hz+ (source needed). That said, I don't have proper testing equipment to conclude this but on the Quntis at its lowest brightness, at 1/16,000 shutter speed on my camera, you can just "barely" see some ultra high frequency flicker. 
3) Asymmetric light path on other cheap models: As mentioned, both of these specific models tested do a good enough job to reduce light glare in my opinion. But I have seen some options do a much worse job at this. So it's just something else to potentially consider on alternatives.

reason: 评论包含固定删除词: first
value:
The USB-C cable needs to be WAY longer on this product, it barely barely reaches to the floor and into an electrical socket for me. 

Also yeah the dial puck should waken just by spinning it, really surprised it didn’t do that, having to press a power touch button is annoying.

Minimum brightness should be lower, for $150 on sale all these things should be ironed out, the USB C cable being so short really surprised me.

Also, this screen bar for the halo 2 does not fit well on the XG32UCWMG, asus’s newest true pack glossy 32 inch 4k WOLED monitor. The large heatsink on the back is just barely too low for this screen bar to clamp on to it, so I have to press it against the back of the OLED screen which just barely reaches.  Also since the OLED monitor is so thin the clamp on top where the light rests is almost too thin to stay there while being stable. It is fine for now but I barely touched it once and it fell face first onto my desk onto my things.  It’s a cool product but many areas for improvement for such a high price. At least it was $30 off for Black Friday sales

I was surprised as well that the actual light bar part isn’t adjustable to rotate it up or down so you can get the lights just out of your eyes, with how tall my ASUS monitor is and how low I like to sit, I can ever so slightly see the LED lights reflection in my eyes. It’s a little distracting, we’ll see if it’s worth keeping this product or not

reason: 评论为无中文随机英文/数字堆砌
value:
Great light, however, it would even better if the minimum brightness could be adjusted lower, especially useful when working at night.   In addition, you should be able to wake up the remote by touching the remote in the middle or by rotating the dial.   Lastly, the numbers for the brightness would be more intuitively aligned if the backlight percentage was on the top, and the front facing light percentage was on the bottom.   I know others have commented on all of these topics, so I'm not alone in these findings.

The workaround for the minimum brightness was to use some 1mil plastic darkened up with a black sharpie/magic marker and tape it over the glass.   It acts like a neutral density filter and greatly improves nighttime usage.   Hopefully you could do a hardware/firmware update to address the minimum brightness in the future.

value:
You're wrong about the blue light. Any light is detrimental to sleep. Blue light is really great and is the most potent wavelength for signaling wakefulness in the brain but when you're working past sundown with any light any brighter than a candlelight, no amount of fiddling with settings will practically help you sleep better other than turning down the brightness overall as much as you can, and any light will interfere with sleep performance. If you want to minmax your sleep you should first of all mount your light as low in your environment as possible, having light eminating from the top of your monitor is not as bad as overhead lights but not as potent as a low desk, or even ground lights due to where the light sensitive cells are located in the eye. 

What it can alleviate to however is eye strain or fatigue if you're sitting excessively in front of your monitor especially in low light environments. But it's not a solution to improving sleep. The literature is very clear on this. You basically need to turn of all lights to properly min max it. Just a nerdy fyi. Blue Light is your friend not your enemy.

reason: 评论包含固定删除词: test
value: I was hoping to get an answer to my question, but since most sponsored reviews aren’t genuine, I decided to buy this Lightbar and test it myself. Skeptical would be an understatement, I wondered about the light distribution and whether it would actually help my eyes. My setup isn’t ideal: a small lamp beside my desk, one ceiling light, and mostly working at night, so I wanted something to brighten the space. I was also curious about the auto on/off function, which was a big selling point for me. To my surprise, it worked perfectly right out of the box. I left all settings at default, and the auto on/off and auto-dimming features exceeded my expectations without a single glitch. The light is fantastic. At night my desk is well-lit, and my eyes don’t feel as tired as before. It’s pricey, but the quality and performance it's definitely worth the $189CA.

reason: 评论为无中文随机英文/数字堆砌
value:
What a beautiful high quality craftsmanship of a product!  Just got it at  $150 and arrived the next day.  Very easy to setup. Package was simple and to the point.  Perfect monitor light size for my 27" Asus Disgno 2K Monitor.   Love that the lightbar comes with it's own power USB C adopter plug and the USB C that's already attached to the back of the screenbar from the back is super high quality.

Love that you can turn down the light to the lowest dim of 1% and have different light color output for the rear light and the main front monitor light separately.  The round controller is of high quality with a very good weight so it doesn't move that easy when using it.  Touch screen buttons are very sensitive and react instantly to the touch, very premium product.  I decided to install the controller to my wall at arms reach for a clean look as the light will not be used constantly and only be used when I need to so it doesn't need to be on my desk. 

Round controller hub is rechargeable and will hold battery for a long time yet it's charges quick.  I you want the light to turn on when you turn on your computer just connect the lightbar to any open USB/USB C port on you desktop or laptop computer. 

Was it worth it?  Hell fk yeah, every penny because it gives a very true to sunlight color light, easy on the eyes and even when I'm laying/relaxing backwards on my LiberNovo Omni chair, the light doesn't hit my eyes.  The back light is perfect for that nice glow to the wall keeping your eyes alert and not making you drowsy and when you do need that full light it lights your front top desk evenly with a full stream of beautiful comfortable light that you can use for reading, fixing stuff or inspecting objects, etc....

When people said that BenQ is the pioneer of screenbar lighting, they were not joking and this newest version is top tear and the flagship for all intimations out there!

reason: 评论包含固定删除词: first
value:
I wanted to try out the Xiaomi light bar first, but almost all of the main sellers on Amazon had poor reviews and higher than RRP prices.

So, I bit the bullet and went for the BenQ Halo instead.

I don't regret my purchase so far, and I find the back light a lot more useful than I initially anticipated.

Yes, you could absolutely buy the Xiaomi light bar for the desk, and then get a cheap LED lamp to put behind the monitor as biased/ambient lighting.

However, I don't have heaps of space behind my monitor and nor do I have a spare socket.

The BenQ Halo handles everything in one solution, which saves space and prevents extra clutter.

reason: 同一工作表内重复评论
value:
If you have a glossy monitor DO NOT get the xiaomi. The BenQ is phenomenal because the light doesn't cause additional glare from a glossy monitor. This is the #1 reason to get the lightbar in the dark especially.

The only thing that really impressed me about the xiaomi over is the non touch controls as the tactile feeling makes a huge difference when using the button in the dark. 

BenQ's backlight is essential if your monitor doesn't have a backlight as the backlight is needed to add depth and reduce eye strain, Xiaomi is front light only. 

I'd say wait for the BenQ on sale, or add some backlight yourself using LED strips and pair this with the Xiaomi. Best bang for your buck.

value:
I’ve cycled through just about every Quntis light bar on the market, hoping to find something that worked. When none did, I gave the BenQ ScreenBar Pro a shot. To its credit, the build quality is excellent, and I believe the advertised specs are accurate. In fact, it outperformed all the Quntis models I tried in terms of light quality and consistency.

But here's the problem: despite the solid performance on paper, my eyes have never felt more strained. The auto-dimming feature in particular was a major issue—it doesn't just adjust brightness; it also shifts the color temperature, which threw off my visual comfort completely.

Thankfully, I’m still within my return window. This one's going back.

Side note: It does look great on the monitor. But if you’re looking for something that won’t fatigue your eyes, you might be better off with an Aputure bulb set to a fixed Kelvin value, placed somewhere in your office for soft ambient fill.
